text.content:
"Ahh, hello. Have you come for goods? Or perhaps... to relieve yourself of souls?"

The hunched Handmaid trades in banked souls (spend from the Bonfire Ledger). Prices are deliberately few — this is a one-shot, not a marketplace. Roll the Shrine Handmaid — Restock table between visits if you want her stock to drift.

Stock

ItemCost (souls)Notes
Estus Shard1,000Give to the Fire Keeper to add +1 Estus charge (cap 6).
Sorcerer's Staff / Talisman1,500A catalyst, if a caster lost theirs or a martial wants to dabble.
Firebomb50Thrown, 2d6 fire in a 5-ft burst (Dex save for half). Stock up before the Pus of Man.
Titanite Shard1,500Pricey on purpose — finding them is the intended path. For Andre's forge.
Ember1,200Kindle at the bonfire for +10 max HP until death (Kindle macro).

Selling: the Handmaid buys nothing useful back — souls are the only currency that matters. (If a player insists on selling loot, value it at the table; it isn't part of the intended economy.)

"Need something reinforced? You've come to the right place. Titanite, and I'll see to the rest."

Andre reinforces weapons and catalysts with titanite + souls. Each +1 adds +1 to attack and damage; the one-shot cap is +3. (Full grid and the scaling system are in the Weapon Scaling & Upgrades handbook.)

Weapon & catalyst reinforcement

Reinforce toCost (cumulative)Effect
+11 Titanite Shard + 200 souls+1 attack / +1 damage
+22 Titanite Shards + 400 souls+2 attack / +2 damage
+33 Titanite Shards (or 1 Titanite Chunk) + 600 souls+3 attack / +3 damage

On a catalyst, the +N adds to spell attack rolls and spell damage instead. Titanite drops from elites, bosses, and the loot tables (or buy Shards from the Handmaid in a pinch).

Estus reinforcement (the Fire Keeper's domain)

Andre can also strengthen the Estus Flask itself if the Fire Keeper kindles it: each Estus +N raises healing by one step (2d4+2 → 2d4+4 → 2d6+4). Optional for a one-shot — offer it only if the party banks a surplus.

DM: applying an upgrade in Foundry

Bump the weapon's flags.ashen.upgrade and add the same +N to the attack activity's attack bonus and damage bonus. Or simply narrate it and have the player add the flat number by hand — the math is identical, and the weapon card's Upgrade line is just a reminder.

"Welcome to Firelink Shrine, Unkindled One. I shall stay by your side... until the day you become Cinder."

The Fire Keeper spends banked souls to raise levels. The full procedure and the per-level choice menu live in the Level-Up Menu Cards journal; run the Level Up macro to spend souls from the Bonfire Ledger and announce the new level.

Quick reminder of the cost curve: 3→4 500 · 4→5 800 · 5→6 1,200 · 6→7 1,800 · 7→8 2,600 · 8→9 3,600 · 9→10 5,000.
